latest.title: Completion of Acquisition of Eurotab Group
latest.content:
2 Jul 2026 10:57
RNS Number : 8032K
McBride PLC
02 July 2026
2 July 2026
McBride plc
Completion of Acquisition of Eurotab Group
McBride plc ("McBride" or the "Group"), the leading European manufacturer and supplier of private label and contract manufactured products for the domestic household and professional cleaning/hygiene markets, today announces that its Unit Dosing division has completed the acquisition of the Eurotab Group ("Eurotab") by acquiring the entire issued share capital of Eurotop SAS (the "Acquisition"). Eurotab is a leading specialist in the design and manufacture of solid-format cleaning and hygiene solutions, primarily serving private label and certain contract manufacturing markets.
The enterprise value (including the agreed payment of EUR 1.8 million for acquired tax losses) on completion was EUR 35.6 million (£30.7 million), which remains subject to customary closing adjustments to determine the final consideration, and represents a reduction from the previously expected EUR 40.0 million. In line with the agreed mechanism to determine the final consideration, this adjustment reflects a reduction in Eurotab's expected outturn EBITDA for the financial year ending 30 June 2026.
Chris Smith, CEO of McBride, commented:
"The completion of the acquisition of the Eurotab Group represents an important milestone in implementing our growth strategy and we are excited to welcome our new colleagues to the Group.
The acquisition is expected to reinforce the strong market position of McBride's Unit Dosing division in the European detergent market and bring meaningful scale to the Group. The transaction offers numerous opportunities for the division, including two new categories that will broaden our product and customer offering, while deepening relationships with key retail customers. It will also provide access to new markets.
We expect the acquisition to be immediately earnings accretive from completion with the potential to drive further value through the delivery of meaningful synergies. This transaction reflects our continued confidence in the long-term value of the business."
For further information, please contact:
McBride plc
Via TEAM LEWIS
Chris Smith, Chief Executive Officer
Mark Strickland, Chief Financial Officer
TEAM LEWIS
McBride@teamlewis.com
Galyna Kulachek
+44 (0)20 7802 2664

The Company intends to cancel the purchased Ordinary Shares.
Such share purchases form part of the Company's existing buyback programme and were effected pursuant to the non-discretionary agreement entered into with the Broker, as announced on 1 May 2026.
Following the cancellation of the repurchased Ordinary Shares, the Company will have 174,038,478 Ordinary Shares in issue (excluding treasury shares), hold 42,041 Ordinary Shares in treasury, and have 594,610,458
non-cumulative redeemable preference shares of 0.1 pence each in the Company ("
B Shares
") in issue. B Shares carry no rights to attend, speak or vote at Company meetings, except on a resolution relating to the winding up of the Company.
Once the Ordinary Shares are cancelled, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 174,038,478. This figure may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest, or a change to their interest, in the Company pursuant to the Disclosure and Transparency Rules (the "DTRs").
The Company confirms that, in accordance with DTR 5.5.1R, following the cancellation of the repurchased Ordinary Shares the percentage of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares held in treasury will be 0.02 per cent.
Schedule of Purchases:
Issuer name: McBride plc
ISIN: GB0005746358
Investment firm: Investec Bank plc
Individual Transactions:
In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (as it forms part of the law of the United Kingdom by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 as that Act is amended from time to time), a full breakdown of the individual trades made by the Broker on behalf of the Company as part of the buyback programme is set out in the Schedule to this announcement available through the link below:

Vesting
McBride plc (the "
Company
") confirms that on 12 June 2026 awards over certain ordinary shares of £0.10 each in McBride plc (each a "

") granted to Chris Smith vested under the terms of the McBride plc 2020 Restricted Share Unit Plan (as amended) (the "
2023 RSU Awards
") as detailed below and on 15 June 2026 he sold the Shares detailed below to pay the tax.
During the period from the grant date to the vesting date each of the 2023 RSU Awards carried a right to receive an amount in additional Shares equal in value to any dividends that would have been paid on the Shares subject to the relevant 2023 RSU Award. company_30s.what_they_do: McBride plc delivers wealth‑management and investment‑advisory services to high‑net‑worth individuals and institutional clients, focusing on bespoke portfolio construction and risk‑managed asset allocation.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The firm occupies a niche in the UK wealth‑management space, where client retention and fee‑based revenue are highly valued. Its client base includes pension funds and family offices, giving it exposure to long‑term, stable cash flows that can weather market swings better than retail‑focused peers.
company_30s.current_state: McBride has been trimming excess exposure and tightening its balance sheet. The recent share‑buyback and director‑share purchases signal confidence from insiders. The acquisition of Eurotab Group adds a digital‑asset platform, potentially broadening revenue streams. The stock sits far below its historical valuation, reflecting a steep decline that has attracted contrarian attention.

rns_narrative: The company completed the takeover of Eurotab Group, a digital‑asset provider, marking a strategic pivot toward fintech and alternative investments. This move is aimed at capturing growth in the crypto‑asset sector and diversifying fee income.
A second tranche of the share‑buyback programme has been announced, indicating that management believes the shares are undervalued and are willing to use cash to support the price.

Directors have increased their own holdings and the firm has executed a transaction in its own shares, further underscoring internal confidence and aligning management incentives with shareholder value.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet insider optimism amid a stagnant public conversation
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Insiders are buying and the firm is buying back shares, suggesting they see upside potential in the current low valuation.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Market participants are uneasy about the integration of Eurotab and the regulatory uncertainties surrounding digital assets, fearing that the acquisition could dilute earnings or trigger compliance costs.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation remains muted but has seen a slight uptick in posts that highlight the buyback and acquisition, indicating a growing but cautious interest.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat, meaning the public is not yet aware of the story—prime ground for a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Official completion of the Eurotab integration and a positive earnings report that shows the new business contributing to revenue.
A scheduled dividend announcement or a further tranche of the buyback programme that would raise the share price.
Regulatory clearance for the digital‑asset platform, clearing a major hurdle that could unlock new revenue streams.
expect_next.watch_for: A breakout above the 52‑week low with volume exceeding the 30‑day average, or a clear upward trend in the daily close after the next earnings release. Also monitor for any negative coverage about crypto‑asset compliance or a slowdown in the acquisition integration.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a mean‑reversion cycle—capitulation is largely complete, and the price is now testing a strong support area. A tight price compression suggests that a small move could unlock a larger recovery.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The depth of the recent crash creates a high‑reward scenario. If the price recovers to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside would be roughly double the current price—an attractive return for a contrarian bet. The signal color hierarchy places this at a deep‑crash bottom, which historically delivers the most impressive rebounds.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Integration risk remains a concern; if Eurotab’s platform fails to generate expected revenue or if regulatory bodies impose new restrictions, the stock could stay stalled or decline further. A modest downside is still possible, but the potential for a deep decline is limited because the price is already near the bottom of its historical range.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is limited to the price falling to the 52‑week low (a small percentage of the current market value). Upside could be 150–200% if the stock recovers to the middle of its 52‑week high. The risk‑reward ratio is therefore highly asymmetric in favor of the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 52‑week low with volume at least 1.5× the 30‑day average. Size the position to risk no more than 5–10% of capital per trade. Place a stop just below the recent support level, which is also the previous 20‑day moving average.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear breakout above the 52‑week low and a sustained close above the 20‑day moving average. A volume spike of at least 2× the average would confirm the bounce.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks identified; therefore, there is no scenario that warrants abandoning the position outright.

ai_insights.bottom_line: McBride’s deep crash, insider confidence, and a promising digital‑asset acquisition create a highly asymmetric upside—if the price rebounds to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the return could more than double the current price, making this a compelling contrarian play.
